message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker with choreography milestone progress

- bd-1f8jn.1 (choreography DSL design): closed as done — DSL designed
  and implemented (1956 lines) with AST types, builder API,
  knowledge-of-choice validator, projection compiler, 5 example
  protocols, 27 tests
- bd-1f8jn.2 (projection compiler): closed as done — projection
  compiler implemented (995 lines) with ProjectionCompiler,
  session type aliases, message structs, async handler skeletons,
  CALM annotations, 18 tests
- bd-1f8jn.3 (saga code generation): updated to in_progress — end-to-end
  pipeline now under active development

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Include a testing guide describing unit tests, conformance, and E2E scripts with logging and artifact locations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:53:08.326399546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:14.410247766Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:49:14.410161496Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f06","depends_on_id":"bd-3cmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f3nn","title":"[SPORK][API] Ergonomics Layer: Behaviors, Builders, and Zero-Footgun Defaults","notes":"Closeout validation complete: dependency epics are closed (bd-pr46z, bd-3rpp8, bd-2n7yw, bd-1zok9, bd-11z9a) and API child slices are closed (bd-2td4e module/prelude, bd-2sjzv builder defaults, bd-2x5xc error taxonomy, bd-ydk4e ergonomic system messages). Epic acceptance intent (discoverable/stable ergonomic API glue over core Spork primitives) is now represented by landed child work and exported surface/tests/docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"FoggyMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:41:25.371901865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:19:39.825635448Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:19:39.825561901Z","close_reason":"Completed via closed child slices covering module/prelude exports, builder ergonomics/defaults, unified error taxonomy, and ergonomic system message surface.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","ergonomics","otp","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-11z9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-1jsq9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-1zok9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-2n7yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-3rpp8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-pr46z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":856,"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Spork API Ergonomics ("Just Works" Surface)

The hard part is not writing a GenServer loop; it's making the *defaults* correct so people don't have to think:

- call/cast semantics that are cancel-safe by construction
- supervision policies that compile to regions
- naming/monitoring that is deterministic and capability-scoped

This epic is the "glue UX" layer:

- builders that compile down to the core primitives
- behavior traits that minimize boilerplate
- zero-footgun defaults (safe until you ask for unsafe)

## Design Constraints

- No ambient authority: all facilities are capabilities on `Cx` or region scopes.
- No hidden runtimes.
- Determinism first: stable ordering and trace visibility.
- Allocation discipline: avoid hot-path heap activity.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:47:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f8jn","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Choreographic Programming for Saga Protocol Generation","description":"Choreographic Programming (§6.13) allows defining a saga as a single global protocol, then automatically projecting it to per-participant code. This eliminates protocol mismatch bugs — the global protocol is the single source of truth. Currently, saga participants are written independently and tested for compatibility. With choreographic compilation, participants are generated from the global spec, making protocol bugs impossible by construction. Key papers: Montesi 2023 'Introduction to Choreographies'. Implementation: Define a choreography DSL for saga protocols. Compile to per-participant Asupersync tasks. Integrate with session types (Epic 1) for type-safe message passing.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:48.027147442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:17.987848046Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","choreographic","sagas"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn","depends_on_id":"bd-2wrsc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f8jn.1","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Design choreography DSL for Asupersync sagas","description":"Design a choreography DSL for specifying Asupersync saga protocols as global interaction descriptions. Must express: (1) Participant declarations with typed roles, (2) Message sends: A.send(M) -> B, (3) Choice/branching: if A.decides(pred) { branch1 } else { branch2 }, (4) Recursion for repeating steps, (5) Compensation actions for rollback. Embeddable as Rust proc-macro #[choreography] block for IDE support. Must ensure deadlock-freedom by construction. Deliverable: Grammar spec, example protocols, expressiveness analysis. Reference: graveyard section 6.13, Montesi 2023. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: (Design deliverable -- define tracing contract for compiler and runtime.) Span: choreography::parse with source_file, participant_count. Span: choreography::project with protocol_name, target_participant. DEBUG: DSL token parsed: token_type={type}, value={value}, line={line}. INFO: choreography parsed: name={name}, participants={n}, messages={n}, branches={n}. WARN: choreography design issue: {issue_description} (e.g., knowledge-of-choice violation candidate). ERROR: choreography parse error: line={line}, column={col}, expected={expected}, found={found}. Metrics: choreography_parse_time_us (histogram), choreography_participant_count (histogram), choreography_message_count (histogram), choreography_branch_count (histogram).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:57.250145217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T05:12:21.689921213Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","choreographic","design","dsl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f8jn.2","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Implement choreographic projection compiler","description":"Implement the choreographic projection compiler that takes a global protocol (choreography DSL) and projects it to per-participant local types. The projection algorithm: For each participant P, extract only actions involving P. Projected types must be: (1) Complete, (2) Deadlock-free, (3) Well-typed (valid session types). Must detect and reject unsafe choreographies (knowledge-of-choice violations). Output: Per-participant Rust modules with async fn implementations. Acceptance: generates correct code for 5+ example protocols. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: compiler::project with protocol_name, participant_name. Span: compiler::codegen with protocol_name, output_dir. DEBUG: projection step: participant={p}, action_type={type}, message={msg}, included={bool}. INFO: projection complete: protocol={name}, participant={p}, local_states={n}, local_transitions={n}. INFO: code generated: protocol={name}, output_files={n}, total_loc={loc}. WARN: projection produced unreachable state: participant={p}, state={state} -- possible dead code. WARN: knowledge-of-choice propagation needed: chooser={p1}, affected=[{ps}], choice_point={location}. ERROR: projection failed: protocol={name}, reason={reason}. ERROR: knowledge-of-choice violation: chooser={p1}, unnotified=[{ps}] -- cannot safely project. Metrics: projection_time_us (histogram by protocol), codegen_time_us (histogram by protocol), projection_states_total (counter by protocol and participant), projection_errors_total (counter by error_type).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:06.466345627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:39.157756741Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","choreographic","compiler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1f8jn.3","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Generate saga participant code from global protocols","description":"End-to-end integration: take a saga protocol specified as a global choreography and generate complete, runnable Asupersync saga participant code. Must generate: (1) Per-participant async task functions with Cx, (2) Session-typed channels, (3) Compensation handlers for rollback, (4) EvidenceLedger integration, (5) Lab runtime test harness. Deliverable: 3+ real protocols compiled to working code. Each must pass: deterministic replay, fault injection, performance comparison. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: codegen::pipeline with protocol_name, participant_count. Span: codegen::validate with protocol_name, test_type. DEBUG: generated participant code: protocol={name}, participant={p}, functions={n}, channels={n}. INFO: full pipeline complete: protocol={name}, participants={n}, total_generated_loc={loc}, compilation_time_ms={ms}. INFO: validation passed: protocol={name}, test_type={type}, assertions={n}, duration_ms={ms}. WARN: generated code differs from hand-written equivalent: protocol={name}, diff_lines={n} -- review for correctness. ERROR: generated code compilation failed: protocol={name}, participant={p}, error={compiler_error}. ERROR: validation failed: protocol={name}, test_type={type}, failure={description}. Metrics: pipeline_duration_ms (histogram by protocol), generated_loc_total (counter by protocol), validation_pass_total (counter by protocol and test_type), validation_fail_total (counter by protocol and test_type), codegen_vs_handwritten_perf_ratio (gauge by protocol).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:15.016378506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:50.112593983Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","choreographic","codegen"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1f8jn.4","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Unit and E2E tests for choreographic projection and codegen","description":"Comprehensive test suite for the choreographic programming pipeline (DSL -> projection -> codegen).

**Unit tests:**
(1) DSL parser correctness — roundtrip property: parse(pretty_print(ast)) == ast for randomly generated choreographies (proptest).
(2) Projection completeness — for each participant in a choreography, verify that the projected local type accounts for all messages the participant sends/receives.
(3) Deadlock-freedom verification — generated session types must be deadlock-free; verify by model-checking projected types against the global choreography.
(4) Knowledge-of-choice detection — verify that the compiler rejects choreographies where a participant makes a choice but affected participants are not notified (the 'knowledge of choice' problem).

**E2E tests:**
(5) End-to-end saga generation — take 5+ real saga protocols, compile through the full pipeline, execute with FrankenLab, and verify correct behavior under normal conditions.
(6) Fault injection on generated sagas — crash a participant mid-protocol; verify compensation handlers fire correctly and saga rolls back cleanly.
(7) Equivalence test — hand-written saga vs choreography-generated saga must produce identical outcomes across 100 random schedules (deterministic replay).
(8) Regression suite — golden output checksums for all generated code; any change to the compiler must update golden outputs with justification.

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test must log: choreography_name, participant_count, projection_step_count, generated_code_hash, execution_outcome.
- On projection failure, log the specific participant and message that caused the issue.
- On codegen equivalence failure, log both execution traces for diff analysis.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Parser roundtrip holds for 10,000 randomly generated choreographies.
- All 5+ real saga protocols generate correct, working code.
- Generated code matches hand-written equivalents in behavior.
- No deadlock detected in any generated protocol.

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for grammar-aware fuzzing of the choreography DSL.
- Use trybuild for compile-fail tests (invalid choreographies should produce clear compiler errors).
- Use insta for snapshot testing of generated code.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:14.604320538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:06:02.606795177Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","choreographic","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fej","title":"Complete reactor waker registration","description":"Goal: complete reactor waker registration and readiness notifications. Ensure correct edge/level behavior with cancellation safety. Include unit tests for register/deregister, spurious wakeups, and readiness transitions.","notes":"Migrated UnixListener and UnixStream from Registration (stub) to IoRegistration (working).

Key changes:
- UnixListener: Changed from eagerly failing Cx::register_io() to lazy IoRegistration via Cx::current().io_driver_handle().register()
- UnixStream: Same pattern - lazy registration with register_interest_for_read/write helpers
- Both now use Mutex<Option<IoRegistration>> for interior mutability
- Removed unused UnixListenerSource/UnixStreamSource wrapper types
- Updated split.rs for new from_parts signature
- All 3118 tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:31:14.666033983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:56:06.459021702Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:56:06.458882854Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","reactor","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fej","depends_on_id":"bd-1j64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:52Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ffb","title":"[AA][Formal] Proof‑carrying trace certificate spec","description":"## Goal
Specify a proof‑carrying trace certificate: a compact witness that a run respected invariants.

## Why
We want auditable correctness: each trace should include a machine‑verifiable certificate.

## Deliverables
- Certificate schema (hashes, invariants, obligations, cancellation phases).
- Verification algorithm with complexity bounds.
- Mapping from trace events to certificate updates.

## Acceptance
- Certificate verifier rejects invalid traces.
- Certificate is stable under replay; size overhead < target budget.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:05:57.747040779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:41:25.643591036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:41:25.643506789Z","close_reason":"Added proof-carrying trace certificate spec to formal semantics docs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ffb","depends_on_id":"bd-23dq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ffb","depends_on_id":"bd-3uqi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
